howItWorks.intro: This court filing fee calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Filing fees vary by court type, jurisdiction, and amount in dispute
howItWorks.outro: Laws vary by jurisdiction and change frequently. This is not legal advice — consult a licensed attorney.

faq.question: How much does it cost to file a lawsuit?
faq.answer: Small claims: $30-100. State civil court: $200-500. Federal court: $405. Divorce: $200-400. Bankruptcy: $338 (Chapter 7) or $313 (Chapter 13). These are just filing fees — attorney fees are separate.

faq.question: Can filing fees be waived?
faq.answer: Yes, courts offer fee waivers (in forma pauperis) for people who can't afford them. You'll need to submit an application showing income below a threshold — typically 125-200% of the federal poverty level.

faq.question: What other court costs should I expect?
faq.answer: Beyond filing: service of process ($40-100), deposition transcripts ($200-1,000+), expert witnesses ($2,000-10,000+), mediation ($500-2,000), and appeal fees ($500-750). Litigation costs add up quickly beyond the initial filing.

faq.question: Is the Court Filing Fee Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard legal matters formulas trusted by individuals navigating legal issues. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

sections.title: What Is the Court Filing Fee Calculator?
sections.body: The Court Filing Fee Calculator is a free online tool designed for individuals navigating legal issues who need quick, accurate calculations in the legal matters space. By entering your case type, amount in dispute, you get instant results including estimated filing fee, service of process, total initial court costs. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting estimated filing fee right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In legal matters, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by individuals navigating legal issues worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to understand your legal situation with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your case type and need to find the right estimated filing fee. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.
